name: Hotels and short-term lodging
summary: Hotels, motels, and short-term rentals for overnight stays.

evidence.claim: FTC Junk Fees Rule (effective May 12, 2025) requires short-term lodging to include all mandatory fees (resort, amenity, destination, facility) in the advertised Total Price.

criteria.name: true_total_price_per_night
criteria.notes: ALL mandatory fees included per FTC Junk Fees Rule (E1).

criteria.name: review_count
criteria.notes: Low review counts should be treated with caution.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Hotel ads frequently understate by excluding resort/destination/amenity fees. Post May 2025 these MUST be included in advertised total (E1).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the advertised nightly rate include resort/amenity/destination fees as required by the FTC Junk Fees Rule?

confabulationPatterns.reality: No standardized meaning. Check review-based quality score and specific amenities.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What specific amenities or certifications support 'luxury' or 'boutique'?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Third-party booking site offering rate >25% below hotel's direct rate
counterfeitSignals.action: flag as potential non-refundable wholesale or bait-and-switch

compatibilityQuestions: Refund / change flexibility needs? Non-refundable rates can be 15-25% cheaper but forfeit on change.
Loyalty program targeting? Third-party bookings typically don't earn chain points or elite status.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Wi-Fi fee (mostly eliminated but some hotels still charge)
